groups.requirements: Implement the SpecIR → code generation pipeline by wiring the existing Tera template engine to actual .tera template files for backend targets (Axum required, FastAPI optional). At least one single-section generator must produce compilable Rust from a SpecIR input. A template test suite is required: known SpecIR input → known correct output. Additionally, add a RequirementPlus → test file scaffold generator: BDD fields (given/when/then, test_type) map to #[test] function skeletons with assertion stubs and traceability comments. A coverage validation report must flag requirements that have no Verifies relationship to any Element. SpecBundle cross-section composition (Phase 2) and cclab-probe integration are deferred unless confirmed in-scope. Hybrid cutover (Phase 3) — templates for verified section types, agent fallback for unverified — is in scope as part of the implementation phase integration.

questions.question: Which single-section type should be the first working generator: `schema` (JSON Schema → Rust struct/Python dataclass), `rest-api` (OpenAPI → Axum route handler skeleton), or another? The proposal lists 6 types but the acceptance criteria only requires at least one. Picking the right first target sets the template authoring pattern for the rest.

Should .tera template files be embedded in the binary at compile time (via `include_str!` or Rust `include_bytes!`) or loaded from the filesystem at runtime from a known path? Embedding avoids deployment friction and keeps templates version-pinned to the binary, but prevents user customization. Runtime loading enables template overrides but requires path resolution logic.

Should Phase 2 (SpecBundle cross-section composition: generator receives SpecBundle, traverses dependency graph, resolves cross-refs) be included in this change, or is Phase 1 (single-section codegen per SemanticType) sufficient to close #932? Cross-section composition is a significantly larger surface area.

The draft test-generation.md spec references integration with cclab-probe. Is cclab-probe integration in scope for this change, or should the test scaffold generator output standalone test files only (no probe runtime dependency) and probe integration be a follow-up?

groups.requirements: Add a `deploy` section type to the SDD section type system. Register section_rules keyword matchers for deploy|container|k8s|docker|helm|terraform|infra|migration|rollback. Implement the section type matcher and define the YAML DSL schema (services with image/replicas/env/ports/health_check, migrations with type/path/run_before, rollback with strategy/auto_rollback_on). Implement k8s validation rules (referenced services/migrations are internally consistent). Deliver at least one codegen target: docker-compose YAML or k8s Deployment+Service manifests. Cross-ref validation: deploy section referencing db-model must validate migration paths; deploy referencing rest-api must validate port/route consistency.

questions.topic: First codegen target: docker-compose vs k8s
questions.question: Should the first deploy codegen target produce docker-compose YAML or k8s manifests (Deployment + Service)? Or are both required to close #934? Docker-compose is simpler to implement; k8s covers the validation rules listed in the acceptance criteria.

Is the deploy YAML DSL shown in the proposal (services/migrations/rollback fields) considered final for this change, or are there known fields to add (e.g., Helm values, Terraform resource blocks, resource limits) before writing the JSON Schema validator?

When a deploy section references a db-model or rest-api section that doesn't exist in the SpecBundle, should this be a hard validation error (blocks code generation) or a warning (proceeds with best-effort output)?

groups.requirements: Implement validators and code generators for the three frontend section types: wireframe (YAML DSL), component (CEM JSON), and design-token (DTCG JSON). Deliverables: (1) DTCG design tokens → CSS custom properties and/or Tailwind config output; (2) CEM component definition → TypeScript prop interface + component skeleton; (3) wireframe YAML → React/Vue/Svelte component tree scaffold (one framework target required). Cross-section composition rule: wireframe + component + design-token → complete UI component with typed props and applied tokens. Cross-ref to rest-api section enables data fetching hook generation in the component skeleton. Validators for all three section types are required before any generator is wired.

questions.question: Which framework should the wireframe → component scaffold target first: React (TSX), Vue 3 (SFC), or Svelte? The acceptance criteria requires at least one. React is the most common but Vue SFC has a cleaner separation that may map more directly to the wireframe DSL structure.

Issue #897 proposes a wireframe YAML DSL. Should this change derive the wireframe validator schema from #897's proposal, or is a different/finalized wireframe DSL spec already in the codebase that should be used as the canonical source?

Should DTCG → CSS custom properties or DTCG → Tailwind config be implemented first? CSS custom properties are framework-agnostic and simpler; Tailwind config requires knowing the project's Tailwind version. Or is producing both outputs from a single pass required for the acceptance criteria?
